skill.name: minimal-run-and-audit
skill.description: Trusted-lane execution and reporting skill for README-first AI repo reproduction. Use when the task is specifically to capture or normalize evidence from the selected smoke test or documented inference or evaluation command and write standardized `repro_outputs/` files, including patch notes when repository files changed. Do not use for training execution, initial repo intake, generic environment setup, paper lookup, target selection, or end-to-end orchestration by itself.
skill.summary: Runs a selected short reproduction command and records standardized audit outputs.
skill.author: lllllllama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak crypto, shell backtick, reconnaissance, and subprocess patterns. Review found the weak crypto, backtick, and reconnaissance hits are false positives from Markdown text, descriptions, or status messages, while the subprocess runner is intentional but risky. The skill is not blocked, but it should publish with a warning because it executes a user-selected local command.

medium_findings.title: User-Selected Local Command Execution
medium_findings.description: The runner accepts a command from the caller and executes it in the target repository with subprocess.run. It uses shlex.split and does not enable a shell, which lowers injection risk, but the selected command can still run arbitrary local tools or repository code.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call and command argument are explicit. The risk is real but bounded by the skill purpose and by avoiding shell=True.

low_findings.title: Repository Path Metadata Collection
low_findings.description: The script runs git status before and after execution and reports changed, new, deleted, and touched paths. This is useful audit evidence, but it may expose repository file names in generated reports.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The git status collection and path reporting are directly visible. The impact is limited to path metadata and supports the stated audit workflow.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak crypto, Ruby backtick, and reconnaissance findings are not confirmed security issues. The cited lines contain Markdown code formatting, descriptions, or status text rather than cryptographic algorithms, Ruby execution, or system reconnaissance commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines were reviewed in context and do not perform the flagged operations. They are documentation or user-facing strings.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run On Caller-Provided Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The script executes the caller-provided command after tokenizing it with shlex.split. This is intentional for reproduction runs, but it must be limited to trusted repositories and commands.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command source and execution call are both explicit. The absence of shell=True reduces command injection risk but does not remove execution risk.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.run For Git Status Probes
dangerous_patterns.description: The script runs fixed git commands to inspect repository state. The command arguments are hardcoded, so this is a low-risk use that supports reporting.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The git commands are visible and use fixed arguments. No user-controlled git subcommand is present in this helper path.

content.value_statement: Research code reproduction often lacks clear evidence for what was run and what changed. This skill runs a selected short command and writes standardized outputs for review.
actual_capabilities: Runs a selected smoke, inference, evaluation, or sanity command.
Captures stdout, stderr, exit status, timeout state, and launch errors.
Extracts simple numeric metrics from command output.
Compares git status before and after the run to identify touched paths.
Supports standardized reproduction outputs such as SUMMARY.md, COMMANDS.md, LOG.md, and status.json.
Records patch-report expectations when repository files changed.
limitations: Does not choose the reproduction target or setup plan.
Does not perform training runs or manage long-running jobs.
Does not verify scientific correctness beyond captured execution evidence.
Requires the caller to provide a trusted command and repository path.

use_cases.title: Verify a README Smoke Test
use_cases.description: Run the documented quick test and capture a concise evidence bundle for review.

use_cases.title: Normalize Reproduction Evidence
use_cases.description: Convert an attempted inference or evaluation run into consistent summary, command, log, and status outputs.

use_cases.title: Audit Patch Effects
use_cases.description: Record whether a short verification command changed files and require patch notes when changes occurred.

prompt_templates.title: Run a Basic Smoke Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use minimal-run-and-audit to run the selected README smoke command in this repository. Capture the result and write the standard reproduction outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Capture an Inference Attempt
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the documented inference command with a short timeout. Record stdout, stderr, exit status, metrics, and any blocker in repro_outputs.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a Patched Run
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the selected verification command and compare repository state before and after. If files changed, include patch state in SUMMARY.md and PATCHES.md.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate a Constrained Command
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute only the approved short evaluation command, enforce the timeout, classify the result as success, partial, or blocked, and normalize all evidence for audit review.

output_examples.input: A README lists a quick smoke command for a cloned model repository.
output_examples.output: The report states the command completed, lists the captured output summary, records observed metrics, and notes that no files changed.

output_examples.input: A documented evaluation command exits with a missing dependency error.
output_examples.output: The output classifies the run as partial or blocked, names the missing dependency as the main blocker, and preserves the error evidence.

output_examples.input: A short verification command modifies generated files during execution.
output_examples.output: The summary calls out the changed paths and directs the reviewer to patch notes for the full audit trail.

best_practices: Use only commands already selected by the main reproduction plan.
Set short timeouts and avoid training startup commands.
Review touched paths before treating a run as accepted evidence.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill to discover which command should be run.
Do not pass untrusted commands from unknown repositories without review.
Do not hide failed, partial, or patched runs behind optimistic summaries.

faq.question: What does this skill run?
faq.answer: It runs a selected short smoke, inference, evaluation, or sanity command for a repository reproduction task.

faq.question: Does it choose the command for me?
faq.answer: No. A reproduction target and command should already be selected before using this skill.

faq.question: Can it run training jobs?
faq.answer: No. The skill is scoped to short non-training verification commands.

faq.question: What files does it help produce?
faq.answer: It supports standardized outputs such as SUMMARY.md, COMMANDS.md, LOG.md, status.json, and PATCHES.md when needed.

faq.question: Why is it marked medium risk?
faq.answer: It intentionally executes a caller-selected local command. Users should review the command and repository before running it.

faq.question: Does it send data to a network service?
faq.answer: No evidence of network access was found in the reviewed files.
